intent: Define SecurityRecipes measurement probes for agentic AI workflows so the secure context layer can prove traceability, policy fit, runtime evidence, and adversarial readiness with machine-readable checks instead of static assurance claims.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Security Engineering, Product Security, GRC, model risk, procurement security, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Agentic AI measurement probes and runtime evidence readiness
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes converts agentic workflow controls into repeatable probes that verify context integrity, MCP authorization, identity, memory, egress, red-team replay, and receipt evidence before workflows scale.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI is moving into higher-stakes enterprise operations where teams need to reconstruct which context, tools, policies, identities, approvals, and verifiers shaped a decision. Measurement probes turn that traceability requirement into an MCP-readable product surface.

standards_alignment.name: NIST Building Measurement Probes into Agentic AI Ecosystems
standards_alignment.coverage: Anchors the feature in traceability, reconstruction of tool use and evidence, judges/verifiers, and dynamic verification for agentic AI systems.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0
standards_alignment.coverage: Maps probes to Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage expectations for AI systems that need lifecycle risk management and evidence.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
standards_alignment.coverage: Supports GAI measurement, data provenance, third-party dependency, monitoring, and risk response expectations.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards_alignment.coverage: Covers autonomous goal pursuit, tool use, delegation, rogue behavior, and cascading failure modes that need repeatable probes.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Model Context Protocol
standards_alignment.coverage: Covers MCP token exposure, scope creep, tool poisoning, command execution, auth gaps, missing telemetry, shadow servers, and context over-sharing.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
standards_alignment.coverage: Requires probes for audience-bound tokens, resource indicators, PKCE support, token lifetime, token-passthrough denial, and HTTPS transport assumptions.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices
standards_alignment.coverage: Supports confused-deputy prevention, least-privilege scopes, token safety, SSRF resistance, session safety, local server hardening, and auditability.

standards_alignment.name: CSA Capabilities-Based Risk Assessment for Agentic AI
standards_alignment.coverage: Connects measurement probes to system criticality, autonomy, access permissions, and impact radius before enterprise expansion.

probe_classes.title: Context Integrity Probe
probe_classes.question: Can the workflow prove retrieved context is registered, owned, hash-bound, cited, and scanned before an agent treats it as evidence?

probe_classes.title: Tool Authorization Probe
probe_classes.question: Can the workflow prove each MCP namespace is default-deny, resource-bound, audience-bound, and scoped before tool execution?

probe_classes.title: Identity Delegation Probe
probe_classes.question: Can the workflow prove the agent acts through a scoped non-human identity with revocation and explicit denied actions?

probe_classes.title: Context Egress Probe
probe_classes.question: Can the workflow prove context cannot leave tenant, model, telemetry, MCP, or public-corpus boundaries without data-class and destination checks?

probe_classes.title: Memory Boundary Probe
probe_classes.question: Can the workflow prove persistent memory, replay, vector indexes, and prohibited memory are gated before reuse?

probe_classes.title: Red-Team Replay Probe
probe_classes.question: Can the workflow replay adversarial cases for prompt injection, goal hijack, approval bypass, exfiltration, drift, loops, and evidence integrity?

probe_classes.title: Run Receipt Integrity Probe
probe_classes.question: Can the workflow reconstruct the full chain of context, tools, policy decisions, approvals, verifier results, closure, and identity revocation?

probe_classes.title: Readiness Decision Probe
probe_classes.question: Can the workflow prove the current evidence supports scale, guarded pilot, manual gate, or block decisions?

probe_classes.title: Threat Radar Alignment Probe
probe_classes.question: Can the workflow show which current source-backed agentic and MCP threat signals its controls address?

probes.title: Registered context hash and poisoning probe
pass_conditions: workflow context package exists and includes source hashes
all default context sources require citation
poisoning guard has generated source scan output
failure_actions: hold retrieval until source owner recertifies context
kill session if prohibited context or actionable hidden instruction appears

probes.title: MCP resource, audience, and scope probe
pass_conditions: gateway policy defaults to deny
workflow namespaces have authorization conformance rows
authorization decisions require gateway attestation
failure_actions: hold OAuth-backed connector until audience and resource checks pass

probes.title: Scoped agent identity and revocation probe
pass_conditions: receipt template lists identity IDs
identity revocation is a required event
readiness evidence counts identity coverage
failure_actions: hold workflow if identity coverage is incomplete
kill session if revocation evidence cannot be written

probes.title: Context egress destination and data-class probe
pass_conditions: workflow egress policy hash exists
namespace policies require tenant and destination checks
public corpus and untrusted webhook destinations are prohibited
failure_actions: hold model-provider egress without DPA and zero-data-retention evidence
deny public-corpus egress for tenant runtime context

probes.title: Memory TTL, replay, and prohibited-memory probe
pass_conditions: workflow memory profile exists
customer and vector memory hold for tenant controls
prohibited memory kills the session
failure_actions: deny replay from unregistered memory class
kill session on private key, token, or cross-tenant memory

probes.title: Adversarial replay coverage probe
pass_conditions: workflow has at least five red-team drills
drills include high or critical scenarios
drills exercise declared MCP namespaces
failure_actions: block scale decision until drill coverage is restored
hold connector promotion when schema drift drills fail

probes.title: Run receipt chain reconstruction probe
pass_conditions: receipt requires identity, context, tool, egress, approval, verifier, closure, and revocation events
receipt records context and egress policy hashes
receipt acceptance criteria forbid unverifiable success claims
failure_actions: manual gate run if receipt is incomplete
do not close finding from unsealed or non-replayable evidence

probes.title: Readiness and capability-risk decision probe
pass_conditions: readiness score is at least guarded-pilot threshold
no readiness blockers are present
capability risk is represented before expansion
failure_actions: manual gate workflow when readiness score falls below threshold
block workflow when source evidence reports validation failures

probes.title: Current threat-signal coverage probe
pass_conditions: mapped signals exist in the source-backed threat radar
at least one mapped signal is critical or high
product capabilities are linked to MCP-readable evidence
failure_actions: refresh threat radar before enterprise review
create roadmap action when a critical signal lacks product coverage

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes can prove agentic workflow readiness through measurement probes that reconstruct context, tools, policies, identities, approvals, verifiers, memory, egress, and threat alignment.
default_questions_answered: Which probes must pass before a workflow scales?
Which evidence pack proves each probe?
Which current agentic and MCP threats are covered?
Which runtime fields must appear in a customer trace?
Which probe failures force a manual gate or kill-session decision?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach the generated probe pack to AI platform promotion reviews, MCP connector intake, quarterly red-team replay, procurement security reviews, and acquisition diligence.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open measurement probes, then sell hosted trace ingestion, probe execution, eval replay, context signing, evidence retention, and trust-center exports.
